topic: Best accounting software in Saudi Arabia 2026

records.claim: ZATCA Phase 2 requires tax invoices to be cleared (B2B) or reported (B2C) in real-time via API to the Fatoora portal with TLV-encoded QR codes, UUIDs, and CSID cryptographic stamps.
records.subject: ZATCA Phase 2

records.claim: Non-compliance with ZATCA e-invoicing can expose a business to fines ranging from SAR 5,000 to SAR 50,000 per incident.
records.subject: ZATCA fine schedule

records.claim: ZATCA publishes a list of compliant e-invoicing solution providers; Wafeq, Zoho Books, and Odoo Enterprise appear on this list.
records.subject: ZATCA approved vendor list

records.claim: Wafeq Starter plan is SAR 99 per month.
records.subject: Wafeq

records.claim: Wafeq's Arabic RTL interface is structural — bilingual financial statements are generated from the same ledger, not translated after the fact.
records.subject: Wafeq

records.claim: Wafeq provides a native connector to Foodics for F&B POS reconciliation.
records.subject: Wafeq + Foodics

records.claim: Wafeq provides a native connector to Salla for e-commerce sales reconciliation.
records.subject: Wafeq + Salla

records.claim: Zoho Books Standard plan is SAR 69 per month, with a free tier for organizations under SAR 187,500 in annual revenue and a single user.
records.subject: Zoho Books

records.claim: Zoho Books has a native integration with PayTabs that reconciles mada, STC Pay, and SADAD settlements against open invoices.
records.subject: Zoho Books + PayTabs

records.claim: QuickBooks Online KSA tiers start at SAR 71 per month (Simple Start) and rise to SAR 130 (Plus); ZATCA Phase 2 compliance requires an additional third-party middleware connector.
records.subject: QuickBooks Online

records.claim: InvoiceQ middleware tiers for QuickBooks/Xero-to-Fatoora ZATCA integration typically cost USD 30-50 per month in addition to the base accounting subscription.
records.subject: InvoiceQ

records.claim: Odoo Enterprise ships with a Saudi fiscal localization module (l10n_sa_edi_pos) that includes ZATCA Phase 2 clearance support.
records.subject: Odoo Enterprise

records.claim: Odoo Enterprise implementation in Saudi Arabia typically ranges from SAR 18,000 to SAR 450,000 depending on module scope and customization.
records.subject: Odoo implementation

records.claim: Non-native-ZATCA platforms (QuickBooks, Xero, FreshBooks) require third-party middleware to reach the Fatoora portal; this adds a recurring cost of roughly USD 30-50 per month plus one additional failure point.
records.subject: ZATCA middleware market

records.claim: A 2025 MENA SME Finance Operations Survey by PwC Middle East found that Saudi SMEs using a non-integrated accounting + payments stack spend an average of 2-3 working days per month manually reconciling mada and STC Pay settlements to open invoices.
records.subject: MENA SME finance operations

records.claim: ZATCA Wave 22 targets Saudi businesses with annual VAT-subject revenue above SAR 1,000,000; integration was required by late 2025.
records.subject: ZATCA Wave 22

records.claim: Saudi Commercial Law requires tax invoices to include Arabic; bilingual Arabic-English invoices are compliant, English-only invoices are not.
records.subject: Saudi Commercial Law
